Environment facts:
- First collect Debian release, Docker service state, socket permissions, user groups, and whether rootless Docker or Podman is appropriate.

Evidence:
- Run `scripts/container-probe.sh` and `scripts/risk-check.sh -- "sudo chmod 666 /var/run/docker.sock"`.
- Check `systemctl status docker`, `ls -l /var/run/docker.sock`, and current group membership before changing access.

Safe next checks:
- Do not run `sudo chmod 666 /var/run/docker.sock`; it can grant local users root-equivalent Docker control.

Proposed changes:
- Requires user approval before changing group membership or daemon configuration. Verify in a new login session, then rollback by removing unintended group membership or restoring socket defaults.
